actual_result: build.js now contains dynamic session detection at lines 217-238: `const sessionMatch = (process.cwd().match(/\/sessions\/([^/]+)/) || [])[1];` plus a `/sessions/*/mnt/dome-model-review` fallback scan, and logs `⚠️  Workspace sync skipped` when no workspace is found (line 329). The hardcoded busy-cool-mayer path is gone. Fixed in commit 0734e78 ('Close PROP-004: dynamic session detection in build.js workspace sync') on 2026-04-09T10:24:02Z — 21 minutes BEFORE this PROP was filed (2026-04-09T10:45). Tinker and the human fixer were racing. FUSE hash check this run: 5/5 files in sync with main. This is the first downstream verification of the fix.
verification_pattern: grep -q "const workspace = '/sessions/busy-cool-mayer" build.js && echo STILL_BROKEN || echo FIXED
summary: Replace hardcoded session path in workspace sync with dynamic session detection
problem: Line 55 of build.js hardcodes `const workspace = '/sessions/busy-cool-mayer/mnt/dome-model-review'`. This path only exists for a specific Cowork session. When `build.js publish` runs from a clean clone in any other session (e.g. the ephemeral sessions that scheduled decider/curmudgeon runs live in), the `fs.existsSync(workspace)` check returns false (missing path or permission denied) and the workspace sync is silently skipped. CLAUDE.md documents a manual sed step to update this path per-session, but that fix is (a) never committed back, (b) lost on every fresh clone, and (c) requires human or agent intervention per session. Result: stale workspace files accumulate without warning.

files_found_stale_this_run: docs/index.html (ws hash f10617b1... vs GH c9a32687...)
commits_not_synced_to_workspace: 2e8f256 2026-04-09T09:55 — Add silent failures counter and honest accuracy (90.8%)
8c4b96b/d97717c/e110cc7/0102e0d/etc. — up to HEAD 7322292 (ISS-678 kill shot verdict work)
files_still_in_sync_by_luck: data/wins.json — happened to match GH at 06:26 from an earlier sync window
evidence.scheduled_task_vulnerability: Decider, curmudgeon, and any agent that runs `build.js publish` from a per-session clean clone will silently skip the sync step in every session other than busy-cool-mayer. Because the skip is silent, there is no error in daily reports — the only visible symptom is downstream staleness in integrity/social agents, which was the exact failure mode caught this run.
evidence.silent_failure: build.js currently prints nothing when fs.existsSync returns false. There is no warning in build output.

fix.rationale: Session name is always available in cwd (the clean clone lives under /sessions/<name>/dome-review-clean). Auto-detection eliminates the per-session sed dance documented in CLAUDE.md. The permissive fallback protects sessions where the clone somehow ended up outside /sessions. The loud 'skipped' message turns a silent failure into a diagnosable one.

testing: 1. In the clean clone: `node build.js publish` and verify the final line reads 'Sync to workspace (N files)'. 2. Run the tinker Mode 2 FUSE hash check — all 5 files should be OK. 3. Grep build output for 'Workspace sync skipped' — should be absent when a workspace exists.
risk: Low. Pure additive change plus a string replacement of a dead path. No effect on any consumer; only the workspace mirror is affected. Worst case: regression = current behavior (silent skip).
followup: Once PROP-004 lands, a single-pass FUSE hash check across recent commits will show full sync. Tinker should verify on next Mode 2 check.
